title: Decarbonizing Aluminum: Rolling Out a More Sustainable Sector
summary: Increased aluminum recycling and decarbonization technology have reduced the carbon intensity of aluminum production by 43 percent, making sustainable aluminum price-competitive with traditional fossil-fuel-based options. The most effective method involves sourcing renewable electricity from hydro, solar, and wind power, with Apple leading upstream sustainability through a $60 million joint venture known as Elysis.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99 percent of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams in aluminum production, allowing the metal to be upcycled and increasing yield while reducing waste. The membrane selectively captures aluminum ions while allowing sodium and other cations to pass through, as demonstrated in lab-scale experiments similar to cryolite waste.

title: Update on the Aluminum Industry Response to Climate Change

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
